title: Perseids at Point Pelee
subtitle: Watch the Year's Best Meteor Shower Under a Moonless Sky

stops.title: Departure from Toronto
stops.description: Meet your fellow stargazers at 34 Asquith Avenue, just north of the Yonge & Bloor subway station. Please arrive by 10:15 AM to allow time for check-in and loading. From the moment we leave Toronto, your guide will begin setting the stage for the night ahead—sharing facts about the Perseids meteor shower, the unique ecology of Point Pelee, and what to expect during our overnight adventure.

stops.title: Point Pelee National Park
stops.location: Visitor Centre, Point Pelee National Park, Leamington, ON
stops.description: Welcome to Canada's first designated Dark Sky Preserve and the southernmost point of mainland Canada. Upon arrival, check into Camp Henry, where your oTENTik—an innovative hybrid tent-cabin structure—awaits. The Visitor Centre provides orientation to the park's trails, facilities, and wildlife. This is your gateway to a 3,700-hectare sanctuary of Carolinian forest, freshwater marshes, and pristine beaches.

stops.title: oTENTik at Camp Henry
stops.location: Camp Henry, Point Pelee National Park, ON
stops.description: Check into your oTENTik—comfortable, park-based shelters that blend the feel of camping with the ease of a cabin stay. Once settled, the rest of the afternoon is yours. Walk the Woodland Nature Trail through old-growth Carolinian forest, rent a canoe at the Marsh Boardwalk, or take the shuttle to the Tip—the actual southernmost point of mainland Canada. Point Pelee sits at the same latitude as Rome and Barcelona.

stops.title: Guided Night Event with Astronomer Caroline El Khoury
stops.description: As dusk settles, astronomer Caroline El Khoury leads the group through a guided night event before the sky fully reveals itself. With her expertise in astrophysics, Caroline will introduce the Perseid meteor shower, explain the mechanics behind it, and point out constellations as twilight deepens. She'll also share tips for astrophotography, whether you're using a smartphone or a full camera setup.

stops.title: Perseids Meteor Shower
stops.location: Dark Sky Preserve, Point Pelee National Park, ON
stops.description: The peak of the Perseid meteor shower—the most popular and brightest of the year. Peak rates reach 60 to 100 shooting stars per hour, with fast-moving, luminous streaks visible to the naked eye. This year's new moon on August 12 means zero moonlight interference and maximum visibility. Far from Toronto's light pollution, Point Pelee's Dark Sky Preserve ensures the sky is as dark as it gets. Lie on a beach at the southernmost point of mainland Canada, look up, and watch the night sky come alive.

stops.title: Morning Exploration
stops.description: Wake up surrounded by nature. Take the shuttle to the Tip Tower for a 360-degree view of open water with the faint suggestion of Ohio on the horizon. Walk the Woodland Nature Trail through Carolinian forest so thick that summer light barely reaches the ground. Rent a canoe at the Marsh Boardwalk and paddle a freshwater marsh older than the country—or book a spot on Parks Canada's Freighter Canoe Tour. Return all rental equipment by 10:45 AM.

stops.title: Bus Departs Point Pelee
stops.location: Visitor Centre, Point Pelee National Park, ON
stops.description: The bus leaves at 11:00 AM. Please pack all belongings, check your oTENTik thoroughly, and meet at the main parking area near the Visitor Centre with all luggage loaded. Our team will do a final headcount before we depart for the return journey to Toronto.

stops.title: Return to Toronto
stops.description: As we drive north, you'll have time to reflect on the night under the stars, share photos with fellow travellers, and catch up on some rest. You'll arrive back at 34 Asquith Avenue by mid-afternoon, ready to return home with memories of one of the year's most spectacular natural phenomena.

bookingButtons.label: Book the Tent Package
bookingButtons.note: Each "Person" is a TENT — you only need one passenger selected to book a tent
